code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    Predicates are turned into readable phrases; triples that share the
    same subject are merged into one sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language phrases.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the object value.
    phrase_map = {
        "alternativeName": "has the alternative name {obj}",
        "bodyStyle": "has body style {obj}",
        "engine": "is powered by {obj}",
        "manufacturer": "manufactured by {obj}",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": "is related to {obj}",
        "transmission": "has transmission {obj}",
        "wheelbase": "has a wheelbase of {obj} inches",
        "builder": "built by {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed on {obj}",
        "length": "has a length of {obj}",
        "powerType": "uses power type {obj}",
        "shipClass": "belongs to the {obj} class",
        "shipDisplacement": "displaces {obj} tonnes",
        "shipLaunch": "was launched on {obj}",
        "shipOrdered": "was ordered on {obj}",
        "shipPower": "has ship power {obj}",
        "topSpeed": "has a top speed of {obj} knots",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "christeningDate": "was christened on {obj}",
        "maidenVoyage": "made its maiden voyage on {obj}",
        "owner": "owned by {obj}",
        "shipBeam": "has a beam of {obj}",
        "shipInService": "has been in service since {obj}",
        "status": "is {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartDate": "has been active since {obj}",
        "shipLaidDown": "was laid down on {obj}",
        "buildDate": "was built between {obj}",
        "cylinderCount": "has {obj} cylinders",
        "totalProduction": "was produced in a total of {obj} units",
        "countryOrigin": "originates from {obj}",
        "diameter": "has a diameter of {obj} metres",
        "failedLaunches": "experienced {obj} failed launch(es)",
        "rocketStages": "has {obj} rocket stage(s)",
        "totalLaunches": "has a total of {obj} launch(es)",
        "assembly": "assembled in {obj}",
        "class": "belongs to the {obj} class",
        "designer": "designed by {obj}",
        "modelYears": "was produced in {obj}",
        "country": "country is {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "founded in {obj}",
        "foundedBy": "founded by {obj}",
        "designCompany": "design company is {obj}",
        "productionStartYear": "started production in {obj}",
        "width": "has a width of {obj}",
        "layout": "has layout {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "parent company is {obj}",
        "operator": "operated by {obj}",
        "product": "product is {obj}",
        "city": "is located in {obj}",
        "successor": "succeeded by {obj}",
        "fate": "was succeeded by {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "key person is {obj}",
        "subsidiary": "includes subsidiary {obj}",
        "comparable": "is comparable to {obj}",
        "finalFlight": "had its final flight on {obj}",
        "function": "function is {obj}",
        "launchSite": "has launch site {obj}",
        "maidenFlight": "had its maiden flight on {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "demonym": "the people are called {obj}",
        "leader": "the leader is {obj}",
        "partialFailures": "experienced {obj} partial failure(s)",
        "site": "the site is {obj}",
        "headquarter": "headquartered in {obj}",
        "associatedRocket": "associated with {obj}",
        "saint": "the patron saint is {obj}",
        "employer": "works for {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "includes the ethnic group {obj}",
        "language": "the official language is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "the leader title is {obj}",
        "anthem": "has the anthem \"{obj}\"",
        "productionEndYear": "ended production in {obj}",
        "division": "includes the division {obj}",
        "shipDraft": "has a draft of {obj}",
        "modelStartYear": "model year started in {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "has a total area of {obj} km²",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "extinctionDate": "production ended on {obj}",
    }

    def predicate_phrase(p: str, obj: str) -> str:
        # Use explicit mapping if available
        if p in phrase_map:
            return phrase_map[p].format(obj=obj)
        # Fallback: split camelCase / PascalCase and prepend a generic verb
        words = re.sub(r'([a-z])([A-Z])', r'\1 \2', p).lower()
        return f"has {words} {obj}"

    # Helper to clean object strings (preserve units in parentheses, remove quotes)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        # Remove surrounding double quotes if present
        if o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"'):
            o = o[1:-1]
        # Keep parentheses content for units (e.g., "120.0 (inches)")
        # Only strip trailing commas and periods
        o = o.rstrip('.,').strip()
        return o

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        # clean the object before storing
        cleaned = clean_obj(t.object)
        grouped[t.subject].append(Triple(t.subject, t.predicate, cleaned))

    # Build clauses for each subject
    clause_parts = []
    for subj, items in grouped.items():
        # Build predicate phrases for this subject
        preds = [predicate_phrase(t.predicate, t.object) for t in items]
        if len(preds) == 1:
            body = preds[0]
        elif len(preds) == 2:
            body = " and ".join(preds)
        else:
            body = ", ".join(preds[:-1]) + ", and " + preds[-1]
        clause_parts.append((subj, body))

    # Build a single complex sentence by connecting related subjects
    if not clause_parts:
        return ""

    if len(clause_parts) == 1:
        subj, body = clause_parts[0]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
    else:
        # Sort clauses by subject for consistent ordering
        clause_parts.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])

        # Build sentence with relative clauses for related subjects
        sentence_parts = []
        for i, (subj, body) in enumerate(clause_parts):
            if i == 0:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{subj} {body}")
            else:
                # Check if previous subject appears in current object (for relative clause)
                prev_subj, prev_body = clause_parts[i-1]
                # Use "which" for smoother connection
                sentence_parts.append(f", which {body}")

        sentence = " ".join(sentence_parts) + "."

    # Capitalize the first character of the final sentence for proper grammar
    if sentence:
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
